style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">NATURE OF OPERATIONS</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company conducts its operations through various subsidiaries and affiliates. The Company has made its disclosures in accordance with United States generally accepted accounting principles as they apply to interim reporting and has condensed or omitted, as permitted by the rules and regulations of the SEC, certain information and disclosures normally included in Consolidated Financial Statements and the notes thereto. These unaudited Consolidated Condensed Financial Statements should be read in conjunction with the audited Consolidated Financial Statements and the notes thereto in the 2025 Form 10-K.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In the opinion of the Company's management, the accompanying unaudited Consolidated Condensed Financial Statements in this report reflect all adjustments (consisting of normal recurring adjustments) that are necessary to fairly present the Company's results of operations and cash flows for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025 and the Company</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">'</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">s financial position as of March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025. The income and cash flows for the periods ended March 31, 2026 and 2025 are not necessarily indicative of the income or cash flows to be expected for the full year.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="margin-top:2pt;text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">WES INVESTMENT</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">WES is a publicly traded limited partnership with its limited partner units traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol "WES." As of March 31, 2026, the Company owned all of the <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-49" decimals="3" name="oxy:LimitedLiabilityCompanyLLCOrLimitedPartnershipLPNonVotingGeneralPartnerUnitInterestPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-343">2.2</ix:nonFraction>% non-voting general partner interest, <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-50" decimals="3" name="oxy:LimitedLiabilityCompanyLLCOrLimitedPartnershipLPLimitedPartnerUnitOwnershipInterest" scale="-2" id="f-344">38.2</ix:nonFraction>% of the WES limited partner units, and a <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-1" decimals="2" name="oxy:LimitedLiabilityCompanyLLCOrLimitedPartnershipLPNonVotingLimitedPartnerUnitInterestPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-345">2</ix:nonFraction>% non-voting limited partner interest in WES Operating, a subsidiary of WES.  In February 2026, in connection with the amendment of certain commercial agreements, the Company transferred <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-51" decimals="-5" name="oxy:LimitedLiabilityCompanyLLCOrLimitedPartnershipLPNumberOfSharesTransferred" scale="6" id="f-346">15.3</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million units to WES, and recorded charges of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-51" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfLongLivedAssetsToBeDisposedOf" scale="6" id="f-347">105</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. As of March 31, 2026, the Company's combined share of net income from WES and its subsidiaries was <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-52" decimals="3" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestmentOwnershipPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-348">40.8</ix:nonFraction>%.</span></div><div style="margin-top:2pt;text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="margin-top:2pt;text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">DISCONTINUED OPERATIONS</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The OxyChem Transaction closed on January 2, 2026 for an adjusted purchase price of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-53" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromDivestitureOfBusinesses" scale="9" id="f-349">9.5</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion, subject to additional post-closing adjustments. In connection with the transaction, the Company retained environmental liabilities relating to legacy sites. Furthermore, there are post-closing indemnification obligations for (i) such legacy environmental liabilities and (ii) pre-closing liabilities of OxyChem, including pre-closing environmental liabilities, in each case subject to certain limitations and procedures, and Occidental entered into a guaranty in favor of Berkshire Hathaway to guarantee these indemnification obligations.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As a result of our agreement to sell OxyChem, the following changes in our basis of presentation have occurred:</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#9632;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.27pt">In accordance with ASC 205, Discontinued Operations, intersegment sales from our oil and gas and midstream and marketing segments to the chemical segment are no longer eliminated as intercompany transactions. All periods presented have been retrospectively adjusted to reflect this change.  </span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#9632;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.27pt">Beginning October 1, 2025, in accordance with ASC 360, Property, Plant, and Equipment (PP&amp;E), depreciation and amortization were no longer recorded for the chemical segment's PP&amp;E and right of use lease assets.</span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Unless otherwise indicated, information presented in the Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements relates only to the Company's continuing operations. Additional information related to discontinued operations is included in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_61">Note 4 - Acquisitions, Divestitures and Other Transactions</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and in some instances, where appropriate, is included as a separate disclosure within the individual Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:ConsolidationSubsidiariesOrOtherInvestmentsConsolidatedEntitiesPolicy" id="f-350" continuedAt="f-350-1" escape="true"><div style="margin-top:2pt;text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">NON-CONTROLLING INTEREST</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company and BlackRock formed a joint venture for the continued development of the first commercial-scale direct air capture facility. The joint venture is a VIE and the Company consolidates the VIE as it is the primary beneficiary. BlackRock's investment is accounted for as an NCI. As of March 31, 2026, BlackRock has invested the entirety of its total commitment of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-54" decimals="-6" name="oxy:EquityMethodInvestmentsCommitment" scale="6" id="f-351">550</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. In addition, the Company has entered into agreements with the joint venture related to project management, operations and maintenance and carbon removal offtake. The Company may incur additional payments if certain construction and operational thresholds are not met.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company may call the NCI on June 30, 2035 or earlier if the plant does not achieve commercial operations or ceases and permanently discontinues operations. Dividends from the joint venture will be distributed preferentially to the </span></div></ix:nonNumeric></ix:nonNumeric><div style="height:54pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">7</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:54pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-342-1" continuedAt="f-342-2"><ix:continuation id="f-350-1" continuedAt="f-350-2"><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">NCI up to a return threshold, then preferentially to the Company thereafter. The NCI receives preferential distributions in liquidation.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"><ix:continuation id="f-350-2">The Company has determined that the appropriate methodology for attributing income and loss from the joint venture is the HLBV method.</ix:continuation> As of March 31, 2026, the VIE's assets were comprised of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-55" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:ConstructionInProgressGross" scale="9" id="f-352">1.2</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion construction in progress. Noncontrolling interest as of March 31, 2026 was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-5" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:MinorityInterest" scale="6" id="f-353">628</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerPolicyTextBlock" id="f-420" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Revenue from customers is recognized when obligations under the terms of a contract with customers are satisfied; this generally occurs with the delivery of oil, NGL, gas or services, such as name="us-gaap:DerivativeInstrumentsAndHedgingActivitiesDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-781" continuedAt="f-781-1" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:98.900%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr><td colspan="3" style="border-bottom:1.5pt solid #00539b;border-top:1.5pt solid #00539b;padding:2px 1pt;text-align:left;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial Narrow',sans-serif;font-size:14pt;font-weight:700;line-height:100%">NOTE 5 - DERIVATIVES</span></td></tr></table></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">OBJECTIVE AND STRATEGY</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company uses a variety of derivative financial instruments and physical contracts to manage its exposure to commodity price fluctuations and transportation commitments and to fix margins on the future sale of stored commodity volumes. Derivatives are carried at fair value and on a net basis when a legal right of offset exists with the same counterparty. The Company may occasionally use a variety of derivative financial instruments to manage its exposure to foreign currency fluctuations and interest rate risks. The Company also enters into derivative financial instruments for trading purposes. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company may elect normal purchases and normal sales exclusions when physically delivered commodities are purchased from a vendor or sold to a customer. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">CRUDE COLLARS</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In February 2026, the Company entered into crude two-way collar derivative instruments beginning in March for the remainder of 2026 to manage its near-term exposure to cash flow variability from crude oil price risk.  A two-way collar is a combination of a sold call and a purchased put. The sold call establishes a ceiling price and the purchased put establishes a floor price that the Company will receive for the contracted commodity volume for a defined period of time. Gains and losses associated with changes in the fair value of the collars are recognized in net sales. The collars have a notional volume of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="mmbbls" contextRef="c-228" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:DerivativeNonmonetaryNotionalAmountVolume" scale="0" id="f-782">100</ix:nonFraction> Mbbl per day, a floor WTI price of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerBarrel" contextRef="c-229" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:DerivativeFloorPrice" scale="0" id="f-783">55.00</ix:nonFraction> per barrel and a weighted average ceiling WTI price of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerBarrel" contextRef="c-229" decimals="2" name="oxy:DerivativeAverageCeilingPrice" scale="0" id="f-784">75.89</ix:nonFraction> per barrel.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:54pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">14</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:54pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-781-1" continuedAt="f-781-2"><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">MARKETING DERIVATIVES</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company's marketing derivative instruments are short-duration physical and financial forward contracts. As of March 31, 2026, the weighted-average settlement price of these forward contracts was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerBbl" contextRef="c-230" decimals="2" name="srt:AverageSalesPrices" scale="0" id="f-785">85.51</ix:nonFraction> per barrel and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerMcfe" contextRef="c-230" decimals="2" name="srt:AverageSalesPrices" scale="0" id="f-786">2.09</ix:nonFraction> per Mcf for crude oil and natural gas, respectively. The weighted-average settlement price was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerBbl" contextRef="c-231" decimals="2" name="srt:AverageSalesPrices" scale="0" id="f-787">59.59</ix:nonFraction> per barrel and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerMcfe" contextRef="c-231" decimals="2" name="srt:AverageSalesPrices" scale="0" id="f-788">2.53</ix:nonFraction> per Mcf for crude oil and natural gas, respectively, as of December 31, 2025. Derivative instruments that are not designated as hedging instruments are required to be recorded on the balance sheet at fair value. Changes in fair value will impact the Company's earnings through mark-to-market adjustments until the physical commodity is delivered or the financial instrument is settled. Net gains and losses associated with marketing derivative instruments are recognized currently in net sales.</span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfDerivativeInstrumentsIncludedInTradingActivitiesTextBlock" id="f-789" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="padding-left:18pt;text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">CREDIT RISK</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The majority of the Company's credit risk is related to the physical delivery of energy commodities to its counterparties and their potential inability to meet their settlement commitments. The Company manages credit risk by selecting counterparties that it believes to be financially strong, by entering into netting arrangements with counterparties and by requiring collateral or other credit risk mitigants, as appropriate. The Company actively evaluates the creditworthiness of its counterparties, assigns appropriate credit limits and monitors credit exposures against those assigned limits. The Company also enters into futures contracts through regulated exchanges with select clearinghouses and brokers, which are subject to minimal credit risk, if any.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div id="i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_67"></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxDisclosureTextBlock" style="min-height:54pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-828-1"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The worldwide effective tax rates for the periods presented in the table above were primarily driven by the Company's jurisdictional mix of income from continuing operations. U.S. income is taxed at a U.S. federal statutory rate of 21%, while international income is subject to tax at statutory rates as high as 55%. The reclassification of OxyChem, which primarily consists of domestic operations, to discontinued operations increased the Company's effective tax rate from continuing operations. The effective tax rate for discontinued operations was <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-1" decimals="2" name="oxy:DiscontinuedOperationEffectiveTaxRateOfDiscontinuedOperation" scale="-2" id="f-860">23</ix:nonFraction>% and <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-7" decimals="2" name="oxy:DiscontinuedOperationEffectiveTaxRateOfDiscontinuedOperation" scale="-2" id="f-861">26</ix:nonFraction>% for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025, respectively.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div id="i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_70"></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:EnvironmentalLossContingencyDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-862" continuedAt="f-862-1" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:98.900%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr><td colspan="3" style="border-bottom:1.5pt solid #00539b;border-top:1.5pt solid #00539b;padding:2px 1pt;text-align:left;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial Narrow',sans-serif;font-size:14pt;font-weight:700;line-height:100%">NOTE 7 - ENVIRONMENTAL LIABILITIES AND EXPENDITURES</span></td></tr></table></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company and its subsidiaries and their respective operations are subject to stringent federal, regional, state, provincial, tribal, local and international laws and regulations related to improving or maintaining environmental quality. The laws that require or address environmental remediation, including CERCLA and similar federal, regional, state, provincial, tribal, local and international laws, may apply retroactively and regardless of fault, the legality of the original activities or the current ownership or control of sites. The Company or certain of its subsidiaries participate in or actively monitor a range of remedial activities and government or private proceedings under these laws with respect to alleged past practices at Third-Party, Currently Operated, and Closed or Non-Operated Sites, in addition to NPL Sites. Remedial activities may include one or more of the following: investigation involving sampling, modeling, risk assessment or monitoring; clean-up measures including removal, treatment or disposal; or operation and maintenance of remedial systems. The environmental proceedings seek funding or performance of remediation and, in some cases, compensation for alleged property damage, natural resource damages, punitive damages, civil penalties, injunctive relief and government oversight costs.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As discussed in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">Note</a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46"> </a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">1</a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46"> - </a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">Genera</a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">l</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, certain Occidental subsidiaries remain responsible for environmental remediation at legacy sites and the indemnification of legacy environmental liabilities and pre-closing liabilities of OxyChem, which were not classified as held for sale. Expenses related to OxyChem and the retained liabilities and indemnification obligations associated with the chemical business are reported as discontinued operations for all periods presented, reflecting the OxyChem Transaction.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-5" decimals="-6" name="oxy:EnvironmentalReservesExceedingTenMillionThresholdValueSiteCategory" scale="6" id="f-895">10</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million each at <ix:nonFraction unitRef="site" contextRef="c-5" decimals="INF" name="oxy:EnvironmentalReservesExceedingTenMillionSiteCategoryNumberOfSites" scale="0" id="f-896">16</ix:nonFraction> of the <ix:nonFraction unitRef="site" contextRef="c-5" decimals="INF" name="oxy:EnvironmentalRemediationSitesNumber" scale="0" id="f-897">151</ix:nonFraction> sites described above, and <ix:nonFraction unitRef="site" contextRef="c-5" decimals="INF" name="oxy:EnvironmentalReservesRangeBetweenZeroToOneMillionSiteCategoryNumberOfSites" scale="0" id="f-898">86</ix:nonFraction> of the sites had liabilities less than $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-261" decimals="INF" name="oxy:EnvironmentalReservesRangeBetweenZeroToOneMillionThresholdValueSiteCategory" scale="6" id="f-899">1</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million each. Based on current estimates, the Company expects its subsidiaries to expend funds corresponding to approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-1" decimals="2" name="oxy:PercentOfEnvironmentalReservesToBeFundedInThreeToFourYears" scale="-2" id="f-900">30</ix:nonFraction>% of the year-end remediation balance over the next <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:f-901">three</span> to <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-263" name="oxy:PeriodOfExpending30PercentOfEnvironmentalReserves" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-902">four years</ix:nonNumeric> with the remainder over the subsequent <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="oxy:MinimumPeriodOfExpendingSecondHalfOfEnvironmentalReserves" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="f-903">10</ix:nonNumeric> or more years.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company believes the range of reasonably possible additional losses of its subsidiaries beyond those amounts currently recorded for environmental remediation for the <ix:nonFraction unitRef="site" contextRef="c-5" decimals="INF" name="oxy:EnvironmentalRemediationSitesNumber" scale="0" id="f-904">151</ix:nonFraction> environmental sites in the table above could be up to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-5" decimals="-8" name="oxy:EnvironmentalRemediationPossibleAdditionalLossBeyondAccruals" scale="9" id="f-905">1.9</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion. The status of the Company's involvement with the sites and related significant assumptions have not changed materially since December 31, 2025.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">DIAMOND ALKALI SUPERFUND SITE</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The EPA has organized the DASS into <ix:nonFraction unitRef="operatingunit" contextRef="c-264" decimals="INF" name="oxy:SiteContingencyNumberOfOperatingUnits" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-906">four</ix:nonFraction> OUs for evaluating, selecting and implementing remediation under CERCLA. Current activities in each OU are summarized below, many of which are performed by Glenn Springs Holdings, Inc.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">OU1 &#8211; </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">80 and 120 Lister Avenue in Newark, New Jersey:</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">An Occidental subsidiary currently performs maintenance and monitoring for the interim remedy of OU1 pursuant to a 1990 Consent Decree for which such subsidiary inherited legal responsibility. In January 2025, the EPA issued a ROD for the final remedy of OU1 that provides for optimized containment for which it estimated a cost of</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">$<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-265" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:EnvironmentalRemediationExpense" scale="6" id="f-907">16</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">OU2 &#8211; </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Lower <ix:nonFraction unitRef="mi" contextRef="c-266" decimals="INF" name="oxy:RiverStretchWhichRequireRemedialActions" scale="0" id="f-908">8.3</ix:nonFraction> Miles of the Lower Passaic River: In March 2016, the EPA issued a ROD specifying remedial actions required for OU2. During the third quarter of 2016, the EPA and an Occidental subsidiary entered into an AOC to complete the design of the remedy selected in the ROD. In May 2024, the EPA approved the remedial design for OU2. In June 2024, the EPA notified the subsidiary that the work required by the AOC has been fully performed in accordance with its terms. The EPA has estimated the cost to remediate OU2 to be approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-267" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:EnvironmentalRemediationExpense" scale="9" id="f-909">1.4</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">OU3 &#8211;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> Newark Bay Study Area, including Newark Bay and portions of the Hackensack River, Arthur Kill, and Kill van Kull: A remedial investigation and feasibility study of OU3 was launched pursuant to a 2004 AOC which was amended in 2010. An Occidental subsidiary is currently performing feasibility study activities in OU3.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">OU4 &#8211;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> The <ix:nonFraction unitRef="mi" contextRef="c-268" decimals="INF" name="oxy:RiverStretchWhichRequireRemedialActions" scale="0" id="f-910">17</ix:nonFraction>-mile Lower Passaic River Study Area, comprising OU2 and the Upper <ix:nonFraction unitRef="mi" contextRef="c-268" decimals="INF" name="oxy:RiverStretchWhichMayRequireRemedialActions" scale="0" id="f-911">9</ix:nonFraction> Miles of the Lower Passaic River: In September 2021, the EPA issued a ROD selecting an interim remedy for the portion of OU4 that excludes OU2 and is located upstream from the Lister Avenue Plant site for which an Occidental subsidiary inherited legal responsibility. In March 2023, the EPA issued a Unilateral Administrative Order in which it directed and ordered such subsidiary to design the EPA's selected interim remedy for OU4. The EPA has estimated the cost to remediate OU4 to be approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-268" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:EnvironmentalRemediationExpense" scale="6" id="f-912">440</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Natural Resource Trustees &#8211;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> In addition to the activities described above, federal and state natural resource trustees are assessing natural resources in the Lower Passaic River and Greater Newark Bay to evaluate potential claims for natural resource damages.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Legal matters related to the DASS (Alden Leeds)</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In December 2022, the EPA and the DOJ filed a proposed Consent Decree in the Alden Leeds litigation, seeking court approval to settle with <ix:nonFraction unitRef="party" contextRef="c-269" decimals="INF" name="oxy:NumberOfPartiesSettled" scale="0" id="f-913">85</ix:nonFraction> parties for a total of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-269" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:EnvironmentalRemediationExpense" scale="6" id="f-914">150</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million for cleanup costs associated with OU2 and OU4. In January 2024, the DOJ filed a proposed Amended Consent Decree that excluded <ix:nonFraction unitRef="company" contextRef="c-270" decimals="INF" name="oxy:NumberOfCompaniesExcludedFromSettlement" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-915">three</ix:nonFraction> companies from the original settlement, among other changes, and subsequently filed a motion to approve the Amended Consent Decree. In December 2024, the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey (District Court) approved the Amended Consent Decree. In its order approving the Amended Consent Decree, the District Court accepted the EPA's revised determination that the Company was responsible for approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-271" decimals="2" name="oxy:EnvironmentalExitCostsCleanUpCostsPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-916">85</ix:nonFraction>% of the cleanup costs for OU2 and OU4. The Company filed an appeal against the District Court's ruling on the grounds that the decision was flawed for several reasons, including the failure to consider the impact of recent Supreme Court decisions that restrict EPA authority and limit judicial deference to EPA actions. The Notice of Appeal was filed in February 2025, and all briefs have been filed as of January 2026.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As a result of the District Court's approval of the Amended Consent Decree, the non-current environmental remediation liability related to OU2 and OU4 was increased by $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-272" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:OtherIncreaseDecreaseInEnvironmentalLiabilities" scale="6" id="f-917">925</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million in the fourth quarter of 2024. This charge was included in asset impairments and other charges in the Company's Consolidated Statements of Operations and represented the additional share of the total estimated remediation costs which may be incurred because of the assignment by the District </span></div></ix:continuation><div style="height:54pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">18</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:54pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-862-2"><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Court of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-273" decimals="2" name="oxy:EnvironmentalExitCostsCleanUpCostsPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-918">85</ix:nonFraction>% of the responsibility for OU2 and OU4. These costs have not been discounted as the timing and amount of the payments are not fixed or reliably determinable. It is expected that the cash outlay for remediation costs will be expended over <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:f-919">ten</span> to <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-275" name="us-gaap:SiteContingencyDisbursementPeriodEstimateAccrued" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-920">twenty years</ix:nonNumeric>, or more.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Alden Leeds settlement does not address the liability of entities that were excluded from the settlement, including for OU2, OU3, OU4 or natural resource damages, or the liability of any settling party with respect to OU3 or natural resource damages. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">While the remedies for OU2 and OU4 are expected to take <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:f-921">ten</span> to <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-275" name="us-gaap:SiteContingencyDisbursementPeriodEstimateAccrued" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-922">twenty years</ix:nonNumeric> to complete, the EPA may seek to require the Company to perform a substantial majority or all of the remediation work and provide additional financial assurance. It is uncertain when or to what extent the EPA may take action to compel further remediation in OU2 or OU4, or the amount of financial assurance that could be required. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In June 2018, the Company filed a complaint under CERCLA in the District Court against numerous potentially responsible parties seeking contribution and cost recovery of amounts incurred or to be incurred to comply with the AOC and the OU2 ROD, or to perform other remediation activities related to the DASS (2018 Contribution Action).  Because costs are being incurred to implement the OU4 Unilateral Administrative Order, a cost recovery action under CERCLA was brought in March 2023 in the District Court against multiple parties (2023 Cost Recovery Action). Both the 2018 Contribution Action and the 2023 Cost Recovery Action were stayed pending the outcome of the Alden Leeds litigation. The Company does not know when the Court will lift the stay in those matters.  If not reversed on appeal, the approved Amended Consent Decree could bar the Company from pursuing contribution against the settling parties for remediation costs incurred or that may be incurred in the future to design and implement the remedies in OU2 and OU4, including claims asserted in the 2018 Contribution Action. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Other information</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For the DASS, a reserve has been accrued relating to the estimated allocable share of the costs to perform the maintenance and monitoring required in the OU1 Consent Decree, as well as the remedial investigation and feasibility study required in OU3 (Newark Bay). Subject to and without waiver of any rights, including appeal, a reserve has also been accrued for design and implementation of remedies selected in the OU2 ROD and AOC, and the OU4 ROD and OU4 Unilateral Administrative Order, based on the December 2024 Order of the District Court approving the Amended Consent Decree described above, which Order is currently being appealed.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The accrued environmental remediation reserve does not account for the possibility of additional remediation costs or natural resource damages for the DASS that are not considered reasonably estimable. The ultimate liability at the DASS may be greater or less than both the reserved amount and any reasonably possible additional losses, and will depend on final design plans, future actions by the EPA and natural resource trustees, as well as the resolution of the allocable share with other potentially responsible parties, among other factors.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The estimated costs currently recorded for remediation at the DASS and the range of reasonably possible additional losses beyond the amounts currently recognized are evaluated periodically. Due to the complexity and scope of the remediation efforts, the estimated costs may fluctuate over time as new information becomes available.</span></div></ix:continuation><div><span><br/></span></div><div id="i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_73"></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:CommitmentsAndContingenciesDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-923" continuedAt="f-923-1" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:98.900%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr><td colspan="3" style="border-bottom:1.5pt solid #00539b;border-top:1.5pt solid #00539b;padding:2px 1pt;text-align:left;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial Narrow',sans-serif;font-size:14pt;font-weight:700;line-height:100%">NOTE 8 - LAWSUITS, CLAIMS, COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES </span></td></tr></table></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">LEGAL MATTERS</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company is involved, in the normal course of business, in lawsuits, claims and other legal proceedings that seek, among other things, compensation for alleged personal injury, breach of contract, property damage or other losses, punitive damages, civil penalties, or injunctive or declaratory relief. The Company also is involved in proceedings under CERCLA and similar federal, regional, state, provincial, tribal, local and international environmental laws. These environmental proceedings seek funding or performance of remediation and, in some cases, compensation for alleged property damage, natural resource damages, punitive damages, civil penalties, injunctive relief and government oversight costs. Usually the Company is among many companies in these environmental proceedings and has to date been successful in sharing remediation costs with other financially sound companies. Further, some lawsuits, claims and legal proceedings involve acquired or divested assets with respect to which a third party or the Company retains liability or indemnifies the other party for conditions that existed prior to the transaction.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In accordance with applicable accounting guidance, the Company accrues contingency reserves for outstanding lawsuits, claims and proceedings when it is probable that a liability has been incurred and the liability can be reasonably estimated. Contingency reserves for matters, other than for tax matters discussed below and environmental matters discussed in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_70">Note 7 &#8211; Environmental Liabilities and Expenditures</a>,</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> that satisfy these criteria as of March 31, 2026 were not material to the Company's Consolidated Condensed Balance Sheets.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">If unfavorable outcomes of these matters were to occur, future results of operations or cash flows for any particular quarterly or annual period could be materially adversely affected. The Company's estimates are based on information known </span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="height:54pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">19</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:54pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-923-1"><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">about the legal matters and its experience in contesting, litigating and settling similar matters. The Company will reassess the probability and estimability of contingent losses as new information becomes available.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">TAX MATTERS AND DISPUTES</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">During the course of its operations, the Company is subject to audit by tax authorities for varying periods in various federal, state, local and international tax jurisdictions. Tax years through 2021 for U.S. federal income tax purposes have been audited by the IRS pursuant to its Compliance Assurance Program and subsequent taxable years are currently under review.&#160;Tax years through 2018 have been audited for state income tax purposes. There are no outstanding significant audit matters in international jurisdictions. During the course of tax audits, disputes have arisen and other disputes may arise as to facts and matters of law.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The IRS is currently reviewing the legal entity reorganization transaction as part of the Company&#8217;s 2022 federal tax audit. Following the acquisition of Anadarko and related divestitures, the Company reorganized its legal entities to better align with the nature of its business activities. This reorganization resulted in the Company making an adjustment to the tax basis in a portion of its operating assets, reducing deferred tax liabilities and recording a $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-276" decimals="-8" name="oxy:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitFromReorganization" scale="9" id="f-924">2.7</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion tax benefit in 2022.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For Anadarko, its taxable years through 2014 and tax year 2016 for U.S. federal tax purposes have been audited and closed by the IRS. Tax years 2015 and 2017 through 2019 have been audited by the IRS but remain open pending the outcome of the Tronox U.S. Tax Court litigation discussed below. Tax years through 2018 have been audited for state income tax purposes. There are no outstanding significant audit matters in international jurisdictions. As stated above, during the course of tax audits, disputes have arisen and other disputes may arise as to facts and matters of law. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Other than the dispute discussed below, the Company believes that the resolution of these outstanding tax disputes would not have a material adverse effect on its consolidated financial position or results of operations. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Anadarko received an $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-277" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromIncomeTaxRefunds" scale="6" id="f-925">881</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million tentative refund in 2016 related to its $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-278" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:PaymentsForLegalSettlements" scale="9" id="f-926">5.2</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion Tronox Adversary Proceeding settlement payment in 2015. In September 2018, Anadarko received a statutory notice of deficiency from the IRS disallowing the net operating loss carryback and rejecting Anadarko's refund claim. Anadarko disagreed and, in November 2018, filed a petition with the U.S. Tax Court to dispute the disallowance. Trial was held in May 2023. The parties filed post-trial briefs throughout 2023 and 2024. Closing arguments were held in May 2024. The Tax Court may issue an opinion at any time. If the Tax Court opines that all or a portion of the original $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-278" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:PaymentsForLegalSettlements" scale="9" id="f-927">5.2</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion deduction is not deductible, a computation phase will commence where the parties will compute the tax amount to be included in the Tax Court's decision. Once the parties submit their computation, the Tax Court will formally enter the decision reflecting the computed tax amount. To pursue an appeal of the Tax Court's decision, any tax due as a result of the Tax Court's decision must be fully bonded or paid within 90 days of the decision's entry. If Anadarko does not pursue an appeal, the IRS will assess any resulting tax deficiency, including interest, and issue a notice demanding payment thereof.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In accordance with ASC 740's guidance on the accounting for uncertain tax positions, the Company has recorded no tax benefit on the tentative cash tax refund of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-277" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromIncomeTaxRefunds" scale="6" id="f-928">881</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. Additionally, the Company has recorded no tax benefit on approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-279" decimals="-6" name="oxy:AdditionalCashTaxBenefitsRealizedFromUtilizationOfTaxAttributesFromDeductionOfLegalSettlement" scale="6" id="f-929">500</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of additional cash tax benefits realized from the utilization of tax attributes generated as a result of the deduction of the $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-278" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:PaymentsForLegalSettlements" scale="9" id="f-930">5.2</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion Tronox Adversary Proceeding settlement payment in 2015. If the payment is ultimately determined not to be deductible, the Company would be required to repay the tentative refund received, plus other cash benefits received related to the $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-278" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:PaymentsForLegalSettlements" scale="9" id="f-931">5.2</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion deduction, plus interest, which as of March 31, 2026 totaled approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-279" decimals="-8" name="oxy:PaymentsForLegalSettlementsInterestAmount" scale="9" id="f-932">2.3</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion. As a result, should the Company not ultimately prevail on the issue, there would be no additional tax expense recorded relative to this position for financial statement purposes other than future interest. However, in that event, as of March 31, 2026, the Company would be required to repay approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-5" decimals="-8" name="oxy:LossContingencyPotentialIncomeTaxExpenseBenefit" scale="9" id="f-933">1.4</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion in federal and state taxes and accrued interest of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-280" decimals="-6" name="oxy:LossContingencyPotentialIncomeTaxExpenseBenefitAccruedInterest" scale="6" id="f-934">997</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. A liability for the taxes and interest is included in other liabilities.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:11.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">INDEMNITIES TO THIRD PARTIES</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company has indemnified various parties against specified liabilities those parties might incur in the future in connection with purchases and other transactions that they have entered into with the Company.&#160;These indemnities usually are contingent upon the other party incurring liabilities that reach specified thresholds.&#160;The Company reserves for indemnity claims when a payment for such claims is probable and estimable. As discussed in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">Note </a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">1</a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46"> - </a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">General</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, Berkshire Hathaway has post-closing indemnification rights in connection with the OxyChem Transaction.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:54pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div In April 2025, Occidental issued <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-284" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="6" id="f-990">41.9</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million shares of stock in return for proceeds of approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-285" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromWarrantExercises" scale="6" id="f-991">890</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. The incremental fair value of the Common Stock Warrants related to the change in exercise price was recognized as an equity issuance cost. The proceeds from the warrant exercise were used to repay near-term debt maturities (See </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_58">Note 3 - Long-Term Debt</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">).</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:13.5pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:54pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">21</span></div></div></div><div id="i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_79"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:54pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:SegmentReportingDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-992" continuedAt="f-992-1" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:98.900%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr><td colspan="3" style="border-bottom:1.5pt solid #00539b;border-top:1.5pt solid #00539b;padding:2px 1pt;text-align:left;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#00539b;font-family:'Arial Narrow',sans-serif;font-size:14pt;font-weight:700;line-height:100%">NOTE 10 - SEGMENTS</span></td></tr></table></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company conducts its operations through <ix:nonFraction unitRef="segment" contextRef="c-1" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:NumberOfOperatingSegments" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-993"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="segment" contextRef="c-1" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:NumberOfReportableSegments" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-994">two</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> segments:&#160;oil and gas and midstream and marketing. Income taxes, interest income, interest expense, environmental remediation expenses and unallocated corporate expenses are included under corporate and eliminations. Intersegment sales eliminate upon consolidation and are made at prices that approximate market. Identifiable assets are those assets used in the operations of the segments. Corporate assets consist of cash and restricted cash, certain corporate receivables and PP&amp;E.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As a result of the OxyChem Transaction, the chemical segment results are presented separately as discontinued operations and corporate costs directly attributable to the chemical segment are included under discontinued operations. See </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">Note </a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">1</a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46"> - </a><a style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i52844973e897496eadafdae90644ac2d_46">General</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> for related disclosure.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Occidental's President and CEO is ultimately responsible for allocating resources and assessing the performance of each operating segment and is the Chief Operating Decision Maker. The CEO may be assisted in this function by other members of Occidental's executive management including, but not limited to, the Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer. While other executives are responsible for the performance of their individual areas, the CEO is solely responsible for allocating resources across the Company as a whole.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Arial',sans-serif;font-size:9.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For both reporting segments, segment income (loss) from continuing operations before income taxes is used to measure performance, as well as allocate resources (including financial or capital resources) for each segment, predominantly in the annual budget and forecasting process. </span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:ReconciliationOfOperatingProfitLossFromSegmentsToConsolidatedTextBlock" id="f-995" escape="true"><div recessions, domestically or internationally; the Company&#8217;s indebtedness and other payment obligations, including the need to generate sufficient cash flows to fund operations; the
